pairs.en: Yeah. Sachin, thank you for that -- so our guidance range breadth is in line with what we had last year and is also reflective of the rapid growth rate that we're delivering as a company. The main swing factors which can all be both positive and negative, I would call out three main ones, one being supply as we've seen in prior years and more supply can make us reach more patients and and more markets. And of course, negative supply fluctuations would impact the other way.
pairs.zh:
是的，Sachin，谢谢你的提问——我们的业绩指引区间宽度与去年一致，也反映了公司目前实现的快速增长速度。主要波动因素可能同时带来积极和消极影响，我主要提三个：首先是供应因素，正如前几年所见，更充足的供应能帮助我们覆盖更多患者和更多市场；当然，供应波动带来的负面影响也会产生相反作用。  

原文：Yeah. Sachin, thank you for that -- so our guidance range breadth is in line with what we had last year and is also reflective of the rapid growth rate that we're delivering as a company. The main swing factors which can all be both positive and negative, I would call out three main ones, one being supply as we've seen in prior years and more supply can make us reach more patients and and more markets. And of course, negative supply fluctuations would impact the other way.

pairs.en: So that's number one. Number two is competition and magnitude of competition. of course, we do not have forward-looking visibility to we see what's in the market today. So it's based on our current read of the markets.
pairs.zh:
所以这是第一点。第二点是竞争及其规模。当然，我们无法前瞻性地看清市场现状，只能基于当前对市场的解读来判断。

So that's number one. Number two is competition and magnitude of competition. of course, we do not have forward-looking visibility to we see what's in the market today. So it's based on our current read of the markets.

pairs.en: And then the third factor is gross-to-net adjustments where we've seen some sizable gross-to-net adjustments in the U.S. over the past few years, reflecting a 69% spread between gross and net sales in the U.S. and that swing factor can also both be positive and negative. So that would be the main three factors.
pairs.zh:
然后第三个因素是总净调整，过去几年我们在美国看到了一些相当大的总净调整，反映了美国总销售额和净销售额之间69%的差距，这个波动因素可以是正的也可以是负的。所以这就是主要的三个因素。

And then the third factor is gross-to-net adjustments where we've seen some sizable gross-to-net adjustments in the U.S. over the past few years, reflecting a 69% spread between gross and net sales in the U.S. and that swing factor can also both be positive and negative. So that would be the main three factors.

pairs.zh:

pairs.en: Lars Fruergaard Jorgensen--President and Chief Executive Officer
Yeah. So thank you, Richard. So just to give a perspective on how we see this. So Martin alluded to that also in the past, have we seen a difference in how patients respond.
pairs.zh:
是的。谢谢Richard。我想谈谈我们对这个问题的看法。Martin过去也提到过，我们是否观察到患者反应存在差异。

原文：Yeah. So thank you, Richard. So just to give a perspective on how we see this. So Martin alluded to that also in the past, have we seen a difference in how patients respond.

pairs.en: That has been based on, say, lower potency products. So we saw a similar -- when we developed Wegovy but with less, say, spread, so to say. And in our view, we have to relate to that when you develop highly potent biologies like what we see with CagriSema, these differences will be amplified. So the fact that patients are different and respond in a different way means that we'll see increasingly as we move up and develop highly efficacy products, you will see this difference in response.
pairs.zh:
这主要是基于那些效力较低的产品。比如，我们在开发Wegovy时也看到了类似情况，但差异没那么显著，可以这么说。而我们认为，当你开发像CagriSema这样的高效力生物制剂时，这些差异会被放大。因此，患者个体差异导致不同反应这一事实意味着，当我们逐步开发高效力产品时，将会越来越多地看到这种反应差异。

原文：
That has been based on, say, lower potency products. So we saw a similar -- when we developed Wegovy but with less, say, spread, so to say. And in our view, we have to relate to that when you develop highly potent biologies like what we see with CagriSema, these differences will be amplified. So the fact that patients are different and respond in a different way means that we'll see increasingly as we move up and develop highly efficacy products, you will see this difference in response.

pairs.en: And in terms of use in the market also to the prior question, I think it's perhaps less confusing for physicians than we believe because they actually use to patients responding quite differently on treatment. And that goes for obesity, but it also goes for any, say, chronic disease that patients respond differently to medicines. So physicians are used to a more patient-centric treatment regime. And I think what really matters here is that we have a highly potent biology that kind of does the job.
pairs.zh:
关于市场上的使用，也回应之前的问题，我认为对医生来说可能比我们想象的更不那么令人困惑，因为他们实际上习惯于患者对治疗反应差异很大。这适用于肥胖症，也适用于任何比如慢性疾病，患者对药物的反应各不相同。所以医生习惯于更以患者为中心的治疗方案。我认为这里真正重要的是我们拥有一种高效能的生物学机制，它能够发挥作用。  

And in terms of use in the market also to the prior question, I think it's perhaps less confusing for physicians than we believe because they actually use to patients responding quite differently on treatment. And that goes for obesity, but it also goes for any, say, chronic disease that patients respond differently to medicines. So physicians are used to a more patient-centric treatment regime. And I think what really matters here is that we have a highly potent biology that kind of does the job.

pairs.en: And the other thing it's up for -- for the rest of us to acknowledge that this is a new sign we get in large-scale clinical development, but it's really linked to the potency of the product. And I anticipate that we see similar signals as we develop equally potent biologies in the future. So I'm quite comfortable with the profile and also that it works for patients and that it will also work in the hands of physicians who are used to more individualized treatment of patients.
pairs.zh:
另一件需要我们在座其他人认识到的事情是，这是我们在大规模临床开发中遇到的一个新迹象，但它实际上与产品的效力密切相关。我预计，随着未来我们开发同样强效的生物制剂，也会看到类似的信号。因此，我对这个特性非常放心，它对患者有效，并且对那些习惯于更个性化治疗患者的医生来说同样适用。

原文：And the other thing it's up for -- for the rest of us to acknowledge that this is a new sign we get in large-scale clinical development, but it's really linked to the potency of the product. And I anticipate that we see similar signals as we develop equally potent biologies in the future. So I'm quite comfortable with the profile and also that it works for patients and that it will also work in the hands of physicians who are used to more individualized treatment of patients.

pairs.en: Yeah. Thank you for the question, Richard. It's important that we reiterate that -- it's our belief that building a sustainable obesity market for the long term is through market access and having patients have a reasonable co-pay and access to the medicine. I'm happy to say for 2025 that we have maintained our broad access for Wegovy, covering 55 million people with obesity.
pairs.zh:
是的，感谢你的提问，Richard。我们要重申一点——我们认为长期建立可持续的肥胖症市场的关键在于市场准入，让患者能够以合理的自付费用获得药物。我很高兴地表示，2025年我们已为Wegovy保持了广泛的覆盖范围，涵盖5500万肥胖症患者。

Yeah. Thank you for the question, Richard. It's important that we reiterate that -- it's our belief that building a sustainable obesity market for the long term is through market access and having patients have a reasonable co-pay and access to the medicine. I'm happy to say for 2025 that we have maintained our broad access for Wegovy, covering 55 million people with obesity.

pairs.en: There were no major changes with opt-ins and opt-outs. It's important to note that these patients have Wegovy available at a low out-of-pocket cost, more than 80% of them paying less than $25 for a prescription. And this is also, in addition, we have now more than 20 states that also cover Wegovy through Medicaid. You also had a question about compounding.
pairs.zh:
选择加入和选择退出的情况没有重大变化。值得注意的是，这些患者可以以较低的自付费用获得Wegovy，超过80%的患者每次处方支付低于25美元。此外，我们现在有超过20个州通过Medicaid覆盖Wegovy。你还问过一个关于复方药的问题。

There were no major changes with opt-ins and opt-outs. It's important to note that these patients have Wegovy available at a low out-of-pocket cost, more than 80% of them paying less than $25 for a prescription. And this is also, in addition, we have now more than 20 states that also cover Wegovy through Medicaid. You also had a question about compounding.

pairs.en: Our latest market intelligence does tell us and show us that it is having an impact, and it is growing faster than we had anticipated. I want to remind everyone that we do not supply compounding, and we have significant actions in place to curtail this. Our focus is on patient safety and educating patients and providers that this is not sema and also to work with the regulators to curtail compounding as well.
pairs.zh:
我们最新的市场情报确实告诉我们并显示，它正在产生影响，且增长速度超出我们的预期。我想提醒大家，我们不供应复合药物，并且我们已采取重要措施来遏制这一情况。我们的重点是患者安全，教育患者和供应商这不是sema，同时也与监管机构合作以遏制复合药物的使用。

pairs.en: Thank you. Just to follow up on the compounding point, for a few months now, all the doses of semaglutide have been marked is available on the FDA drug shortage website, but the molecule is still marked as in shortage. When do you expect that to be removed? And then would that lead to a similar kind of off-ramp from the FDA for the compounders that we saw FDA issued a directive in December for trazepatide? And then secondly, another question on REDEFINE 1. The red curve for the patients that were on the lower dose at the end of treatment, is it fair to say that a significant number of those patients did go up to 2.4 milligram and then titrated down whether for tolerability or that they were very, very fast responders to the weight loss.
pairs.zh:
谢谢。我想跟进关于复合制剂的问题。几个月来，semaglutide 的所有剂量都已标记为在 FDA 药品短缺网站上可用，但该分子仍被标记为短缺。您预计何时会移除这一标记？之后是否会导致 FDA 对复合制剂生产商采取类似的退出机制，就像去年12月 FDA 对 trazepatide 发布的指令那样？其次，另一个关于 REDEFINE 1 的问题。对于治疗结束时处于较低剂量组的患者，他们的红色曲线——是否可以说其中相当一部分患者确实增加到了 2.4 毫克，然后由于耐受性或对体重减轻反应非常快而逐渐减量？

原文：
Thank you. Just to follow up on the compounding point, for a few months now, all the doses of semaglutide have been marked is available on the FDA drug shortage website, but the molecule is still marked as in shortage. When do you expect that to be removed? And then would that lead to a similar kind of off-ramp from the FDA for the compounders that we saw FDA issued a directive in December for trazepatide? The red curve for the patients that were on the lower dose at the end of treatment, is it fair to say that a significant number of those patients did go up to 2.4 milligram and then titrated down whether for tolerability or that they were very, very fast responders to the weight loss.

pairs.en: Yeah. Absolutely. I want to go back to Lars' point that obesity is a complex disease. And patients have individual response to treatment.
pairs.zh:
是的，完全同意。我想回到Lars的观点，肥胖是一种复杂的疾病。患者对治疗有各自的反应。

Yeah. Absolutely. I want to go back to Lars' point that obesity is a complex disease. And patients have individual response to treatment.

pairs.en: So in the group where patients did not titrate to full dose at end of trial, the mean dose at week 20 was around 1.5 milligrams, indicating that very few actually opted to try to try to full 2.4 milligram dose. This is more to be seen as a group of fast and high response. And therefore, with the weight loss that they accrued, which was then also faster than the other group, they started to slow down to balance the speed of their weight loss, their gastrointestinal side effects, but also the fact that they were approaching a level below the definition of obesity. And therefore, again, it speaks to the very powerful biology that we see, but also the need to individualize treatment.
pairs.zh:
因此，在试验结束时未将剂量滴定至全剂量的患者组中，第20周的平均剂量约为1.5毫克，表明实际上很少有人尝试达到2.4毫克的全剂量。这更多地被视为一组快速且高反应的患者。因此，随着他们累积的体重减轻——其速度也快于另一组——他们开始减缓减重速度以平衡体重下降的速度、胃肠道副作用，以及他们正接近低于肥胖定义水平的事实。因此，这再次说明了我们观察到的非常强大的生物学效应，同时也凸显了个体化治疗的必要性。

So in the group where patients did not titrate to full dose at end of trial, the mean dose at week 20 was around 1.5 milligrams, indicating that very few actually opted to try to try to full 2.4 milligram dose. This is more to be seen as a group of fast and high response. And therefore, with the weight loss that they accrued, which was then also faster than the other group, they started to slow down to balance the speed of their weight loss, their gastrointestinal side effects, but also the fact that they were approaching a level below the definition of obesity. And therefore, again, it speaks to the very powerful biology that we see, but also the need to individualize treatment.

pairs.en: And again, I'll just remind you, this is the trial at a population level where we've seen very few gastrointestinal side effects at the level of Wegovy. And therefore, that is not the key driver of how patients choose to titrate, right? I think Lars has a really good point. Patients know how to do this together with their physicians.
pairs.zh:
再次提醒您，这是在人群水平上的试验，我们在Wegovy剂量水平上观察到极少的胃肠道副作用。因此，这并不是患者选择如何调整剂量的关键驱动因素，对吧？我认为Lars说得很有道理。患者知道如何与医生共同完成这件事。

And again, I'll just remind you, this is the trial at a population level where we've seen very few gastrointestinal side effects at the level of Wegovy. And therefore, that is not the key driver of how patients choose to titrate, right? I think Lars has a really good point. Patients know how to do this together with their physicians.

pairs.en: Thank you very much for that question. So to remind us, one being we never did the acquisition of Boludabant to develop it purely for diabetic kidney disease. Our focus was on the weight loss potential. Second, I'll just remind you that these are small studies.
pairs.zh:
非常感谢您的提问。首先提醒我们，第一，我们从未收购Boludabant以纯粹开发用于糖尿病肾病。我们的重点在于其减重潜力。第二，我要提醒您，这些都是小型研究。

Thank you very much for that question. So to remind us, one being we never did the acquisition of Boludabant to develop it purely for diabetic kidney disease. Our focus was on the weight loss potential. Second, I'll just remind you that these are small studies.

pairs.en: So obviously, we try to see them in the context of the full picture. So we are not discouraged by the fact that we did not see impact on the actual diabetic kidney disease. That study did confirm a weight loss potential for molunobant. And when we look at the safety and tolerability profile, it was comparable, albeit with slightly lower rates than in the dedicated obesity story, basically indicating that we can still have an aspiration of exploring this further in Phase 2b with lower doses looking at weight loss potential but obviously also -- and this has been the attempt from the get go, also ruling out an ability -- a potential safety concern.
pairs.zh:
显然，我们会从全局视角来审视这些数据。因此，我们并未因在糖尿病肾病方面未观察到效果而气馁。这项研究确实证实了molunobant具有减重潜力。从安全性和耐受性来看，其结果与专门针对肥胖症的研究相当，但发生率略低，这基本表明我们仍有望在2b期临床试验中以较低剂量进一步探索其减重潜力。当然——这也是我们从一开始就尝试的方向——还需要排除潜在的安全隐患。

原文：
So obviously, we try to see them in the context of the full picture. So we are not discouraged by the fact that we did not see impact on the actual diabetic kidney disease. That study did confirm a weight loss potential for molunobant. And when we look at the safety and tolerability profile, it was comparable, albeit with slightly lower rates than in the dedicated obesity story, basically indicating that we can still have an aspiration of exploring this further in Phase 2b with lower doses looking at weight loss potential but obviously also -- and this has been the attempt from the get go, also ruling out an ability -- a potential safety concern.

pairs.en: Lars Fruergaard Jorgensen--President and Chief Executive Officer

Thank you, Evan, for bringing that up. I think it's really be a good topic to discuss. And obviously, it's a quite broad topic. And I think it -- the discussion opens up with what we now see in terms of this case's biologies because you say that with former generation products like Wegovy, you could, in principle, say, load patients up and all would say tolerate the weight loss they see.
pairs.zh:

原文：
Thank you, Evan, for bringing that up. I think it's really be a good topic to discuss. And obviously, it's a quite broad topic. And I think it -- the discussion opens up with what we now see in terms of this case's biologies because you say that with former generation products like Wegovy, you could, in principle, say, load patients up and all would say tolerate the weight loss they see.

pairs.en: And also the GI tolerability, we know, is very good. But when you get into, say, the next-generation products, where you amplify the weight loss, you tease out the difference between different patients who we are still trying to look into the omics and figure out what defines the difference. And we have a lot of data. So we can start actually finding ideas about who will respond in certain ways and from a speed of weight loss, etc.
pairs.zh:
而且，胃肠道耐受性，我们知道，是非常好的。但当你进入，比如说，下一代产品，其中放大了减重效果，你就能区分出不同患者之间的差异——我们仍在通过组学研究，试图找出决定这些差异的因素。我们拥有大量数据。因此我们可以开始真正找到一些思路，了解哪些患者会以特定方式产生反应，以及从减重速度等方面进行分析。

And also the GI tolerability, we know, is very good. But when you get into, say, the next-generation products, where you amplify the weight loss, you tease out the difference between different patients who we are still trying to look into the omics and figure out what defines the difference. And we have a lot of data. So we can start actually finding ideas about who will respond in certain ways and from a speed of weight loss, etc.

pairs.en: So that -- then we're into the topic of, say, quality of weight loss. And I think in the early days of obesity, we have all been obsessed by the percentage over time. And I think that's a problematic ratio because if you have lived with obesity, a good part of your life and suddenly, you lose, say 25%, in some even more percent weight loss in a matter of, say, half year to a year, that's a very, very dramatic, say, change in your life and not necessarily what anyone would like. So that's one.
pairs.zh:
那么——我们就进入了减肥质量的话题。我认为在肥胖问题的早期，我们都过分关注随时间变化的百分比。这是一个有问题的比率，因为如果你长期患有肥胖症，突然在半年到一年内减掉25%甚至更多的体重，这在你生活中是一个非常戏剧性的变化，而且未必是任何人想要的。所以这是第一点。

原文：So that -- then we're into the topic of, say, quality of weight loss. And I think in the early days of obesity, we have all been obsessed by the percentage over time. And I think that's a problematic ratio because if you have lived with obesity, a good part of your life and suddenly, you lose, say 25%, in some even more percent weight loss in a matter of, say, half year to a year, that's a very, very dramatic, say, change in your life and not necessarily what anyone would like. So that's one.

pairs.en: And then, of course, we have all the comorbidities. And increasingly, I think with the establishment of CV benefits, liver benefits, etc., it also becomes a matter of, say, the health outcome improvements you have. So in this, say, opportunity space, I think it's important to be able to address those opportunities with different type of agents, to cater for these differences. Short term, as I mentioned before, I think patients together with the physician are quite comfortable in managing this journey.
pairs.zh:
当然，我们还有所有的合并症。而且我认为，随着心血管获益、肝脏获益等效果的确认，这越来越关乎你所实现的健康结果改善。因此，在这个机遇空间中，我认为能够使用不同类型的药物来应对这些机遇、适应这些差异非常重要。短期来看，正如我之前提到的，我认为患者和医生在管理这一过程中是相当自如的。

And then, of course, we have all the comorbidities. And increasingly, I think with the establishment of CV benefits, liver benefits, etc., it also becomes a matter of, say, the health outcome improvements you have. So in this, say, opportunity space, I think it's important to be able to address those opportunities with different type of agents, to cater for these differences. Short term, as I mentioned before, I think patients together with the physician are quite comfortable in managing this journey.

pairs.en: And I think we are perhaps struggling a bit in doing the perfect segmentation of what this market will look like. But I think we can look into all the data we have and find ways to also more targeted direct specific products to certain subsegments. So I think this is another example of the fact that we're in the early days of understanding obesity, how patients are different. And I think it's all opportunity for us with the breadth of the portfolio we have and all the data we have.
pairs.zh:
我认为我们在对这个市场的完美细分上可能还有些挣扎。但我觉得我们可以利用所有已有的数据，找到更精准地针对某些子领域推出特定产品的方法。所以我认为这再次证明了我们仍处于理解肥胖症及患者个体差异的早期阶段。而凭借我们广泛的组合和所有数据，这一切都是机遇。

And I think we are perhaps struggling a bit in doing the perfect segmentation of what this market will look like. But I think we can look into all the data we have and find ways to also more targeted direct specific products to certain subsegments. So I think this is another example of the fact that we're in the early days of understanding obesity, how patients are different. And I think it's all opportunity for us with the breadth of the portfolio we have and all the data we have.

pairs.en: So yes, percentage of weight loss matters what quality of weight loss and benefit on comorbidities, etc., etc., also matters. And it's a net total equation that I think we have a really exciting opportunity for continuous leadership in the space.
pairs.zh:
所以是的，减重的百分比很重要，减重的质量以及对合并症等的益处也很重要。这是一个净总方程式，我认为我们在该领域拥有一个非常令人兴奋的持续领先机会。

So yes, percentage of weight loss matters what quality of weight loss and benefit on comorbidities, etc., etc., also matters. And it's a net total equation that I think we have a really exciting opportunity for continuous leadership in the space.

Do you feel that you have enough data to progress into Phase 3? Or would perhaps another Phase 2 be advisable?
pairs.zh:

So when I pulled together your comments on CagriSema, the high potency, individual patient responses and then we layer in the proposed FDA guidelines that say Phase 2 data should be sufficient to capture maximal or new maximal weight reduction effects with the dosing regime. Do you feel that you have enough data to progress into Phase 3? Or would perhaps another Phase 2 be advisable?

pairs.en: Yeah. So first of all, we had these two very clear distinct groups. The larger group of 57% of the people titrating to 2.4 milligram. Just to give you an example, they were at a mean dose of 2.2 milligram at 20 weeks.
pairs.zh:
是的。首先，我们有两个非常明确的群体。较大的一组占57%，他们逐步增加剂量至2.4毫克。举个例子，他们在20周时的平均剂量为2.2毫克。

原文：Yeah. So first of all, we had these two very clear distinct groups. The larger group of 57% of the people titrating to 2.4 milligram. Just to give you an example, they were at a mean dose of 2.2 milligram at 20 weeks.

pairs.en: And they then continue to the full 2.4 milligram% and appear to stay on that. There were a few patients doing ups and downs, but it would not be meaningful to try to tease them out. Similarly, I just want to remind you, at week 20, the other group were at 1.5 milligram and they down titrated a little bit as a group to add Endotrial 1.1 while the -- while basically securing a weight loss that was higher than what we've seen before, namely 25.2%. At the same time, this way of allowing patients to do -- I don't want to say personal titration but close to actually allowed us to see the lowest overall dropout ever seen in a Phase 3 pivotal trial but also very low, and again, the lowest gastrointestinal dropouts seen in the pivotal trial.
pairs.zh:
然后他们继续服用至完整的2.4毫克%，并似乎维持在该剂量。有少数患者出现了剂量波动，但试图将其分离出来并无意义。同样，我想提醒您，在第20周时，另一组患者的剂量为1.5毫克，他们作为整体略微下调了剂量，同时加入了Endotrial 1.1，而基本上确保了比此前更高的减重效果，即25.2%。与此同时，这种允许患者自行调整的方式——我不想称之为个体化剂量调整，但已接近于此——实际上让我们看到了三期关键试验中最低的总退出率，同样也非常低的胃肠道退出率，再次成为关键试验中最低的。

And they then continue to the full 2.4 milligram% and appear to stay on that. There were a few patients doing ups and downs, but it would not be meaningful to try to tease them out. Similarly, I just want to remind you, at week 20, the other group were at 1.5 milligram and they down titrated a little bit as a group to add Endotrial 1.1 while the -- while basically securing a weight loss that was higher than what we've seen before, namely 25.2%. At the same time, this way of allowing patients to do -- I don't want to say personal titration but close to actually allowed us to see the lowest overall dropout ever seen in a Phase 3 pivotal trial but also very low, and again, the lowest gastrointestinal dropouts seen in the pivotal trial.

pairs.en: And just a reminder, CagriSema in REDEFINE 1, 3.6% dropout; semaglutide in step 1, 4.5% dropout due to gastrointestinal side effects. So we're actually quite encouraged by the data. And as Lars also alluded to, by employing this individual approach to patients moving forward, we can really leverage the full benefit of not only CagriSema but also our pipeline products.
pairs.zh:
提醒一下，在REDEFINE 1试验中，CagriSema的脱落率为3.6%；在step 1试验中，semaglutide因胃肠道副作用导致的脱落率为4.5%。因此，我们对这些数据感到相当鼓舞。正如Lars所提到的，未来通过对患者采用这种个体化方法，我们能够真正充分利用CagriSema以及我们产品线的全部潜力。

And just a reminder, CagriSema in REDEFINE 1, 3.6% dropout; semaglutide in step 1, 4.5% dropout due to gastrointestinal side effects. So we're actually quite encouraged by the data. And as Lars also alluded to, by employing this individual approach to patients moving forward, we can really leverage the full benefit of not only CagriSema but also our pipeline products.

pairs.en: Lars Fruergaard Jorgensen--President and Chief Executive Officer

Thank you, Jacob. I'm very pleased with the 2024 sales growth of 26%, driven by our GLP-1 portfolio in both operating units. And within R&D, we see a strong momentum, as we just discussed in our pipeline, is underlined by the recent readouts in obesity, both for CagriSema and amycretin. And of course, I'm also very pleased with the expected 2025 outlook.
pairs.zh:
谢谢你，Jacob。我对2024年销售额增长26%感到非常满意，这得益于我们两个业务部门的GLP-1产品组合。在研发方面，我们看到强劲势头，正如我们刚刚讨论的管线，近期在肥胖领域针对CagriSema和amycretin的读出数据进一步印证了这一点。当然，我对预期的2025年展望也感到非常满意。

原文：Thank you, Jacob. I'm very pleased with the 2024 sales growth of 26%, driven by our GLP-1 portfolio in both operating units. And within R&D, we see a strong momentum, as we just discussed in our pipeline, is underlined by the recent readouts in obesity, both for CagriSema and amycretin. And of course, I'm also very pleased with the expected 2025 outlook.

pairs.en: We continue to focus heavily on commercial execution and on the progression of our R&D pipeline and the expansion of our production capacity. So the plan is very clear, and we know what it takes to execute on this. So also thank you from me on behalf of management on your time today. We appreciate the opportunity to discuss our business with you.
pairs.zh:
我们继续高度关注商业执行、研发管线的推进以及生产能力的扩展。因此，计划非常明确，我们清楚需要如何执行。我也代表管理层感谢您今天拨冗出席。我们很珍惜与您讨论业务的机会。

We continue to focus heavily on commercial execution and on the progression of our R&D pipeline and the expansion of our production capacity. So the plan is very clear, and we know what it takes to execute on this. So also thank you from me on behalf of management on your time today. We appreciate the opportunity to discuss our business with you.

pairs.en: Thank you very much. Bye-bye.
pairs.zh:
非常感谢。再见。

Thank you very much. Bye-bye.
